_doc: Encoded theses for the Signal Tracker (packages/signals), analogous to data/tickers.json. Each thesis: an id, a plain-English claim, the tickers/entities it depends on, and watch-items with tripwire thresholds. `sources` lists which Event sources can satisfy a watch-item; `feed` is 'auto' (a feed adapter populates it) or 'manual' (hand-entered Event required — used where our FMP Starter tier / EDGAR doesn't cover the source, e.g. foreign filers like TSMC). Validated by ThesesFile in packages/schema. Phase 1 only ingests + diffs events; tripwire evaluation is Phase 2/3.

theses.claim: NVIDIA sustains data-center gross margins in the low-to-mid 70s% as competition (custom silicon, AMD) and customer concentration pressure pricing; margin compression below the low-70s would break the premium-valuation case.

watchItems.label: Forward gross-margin guidance direction (8-K Ex-99.1 / 10-Q MD&A / earnings call)
watchItems.tripwire: Guided or reported non-GAAP gross margin falls below 72%, or guidance language flags sustained pricing pressure.

watchItems.label: Consensus FY EPS revision (are estimates catching up / rolling over?)
watchItems.tripwire: Forward-year consensus EPS revised DOWN, or analyst bull-index drops >10 month-over-month (sentiment rolling over).

theses.claim: The AI-infrastructure build-out is still accelerating, not plateauing: hyperscaler capex guidance keeps rising and the supply chain (TSMC, Micron HBM) stays sold out. A capex-guide cut or an HBM-glut signal would mark the cycle top.

watchItems.label: Hyperscaler capex guidance vs prior quarter (MSFT/GOOGL/AMZN 8-K / 10-Q)
watchItems.tripwire: Any hyperscaler guides capex FLAT or DOWN sequentially, or explicitly signals a build-out pause.

watchItems.label: Micron HBM sold-out window (10-Q MD&A / 8-K)
watchItems.tripwire: Micron stops extending the HBM sold-out window, or language softens from 'sold out' to 'strong demand'.

watchItems.label: TSMC capex guide direction (MANUAL — foreign filer, not on EDGAR/Starter)
watchItems.tripwire: TSMC cuts or holds its annual capex guide vs prior guide (proxy for leading-edge demand topping).

theses.claim: Google's TPU external-revenue opportunity (selling/renting TPUs beyond internal use) is not yet in sell-side consensus; first evidence of material external TPU revenue would be a non-consensus positive. Once consensus prices it in, the edge is gone.

watchItems.label: TPU external-revenue disclosure in filings or on the earnings call
watchItems.tripwire: Google first discloses or quantifies external/third-party TPU revenue in a filing.

watchItems.label: Consensus / ratings catching up to the TPU story
watchItems.tripwire: Consensus revenue revised UP sharply or bull-index rises >10 month-over-month AFTER a TPU disclosure (edge getting priced in — thesis becomes consensus).

theses.claim: DRAM/memory is the best risk-adjusted growth exposure despite an elevated P/E: AI-driven HBM and high-density DRAM demand keeps outpacing supply, so the premium multiple is justified while the up-cycle runs. The exit trigger is the memory PRICING CYCLE rolling over — declining DRAM average selling prices, softening Micron guidance, or supply/inventory-glut signals (from Micron or the Korean majors) mark the top, at which point exit DRAM and the sector. Tracked via Micron (MU) as the US-listed pure-play proxy; consensus-revision feeds are unavailable for MU on our FMP tier, so the cycle is read directly from filings and earnings calls (the better leading indicator anyway).

watchItems.label: DRAM average selling price (ASP) / bit-demand trend (MU 8-K Ex-99.1 / 10-Q MD&A / earnings call)
watchItems.tripwire: Micron reports or guides DECLINING DRAM ASPs (sequential price erosion) or decelerating bit-demand growth — the classic leading indicator that the memory pricing cycle is rolling over.

watchItems.label: Micron next-quarter revenue / gross-margin guidance direction (8-K Ex-99.1 / 10-Q MD&A / earnings call)
watchItems.tripwire: Micron guides next-quarter revenue OR gross margin DOWN sequentially, or guidance language shifts from supply-constrained ('sold out', 'tight') to demand-softening ('inventory adjustment', 'softer demand').

watchItems.label: Memory supply discipline — capex / wafer capacity & customer inventory (MU 10-Q MD&A / 8-K / earnings call)
watchItems.tripwire: Micron signals a capex INCREASE / wafer-capacity expansion that risks oversupply, or flags RISING customer/channel inventory — an early warning of a supply glut that depresses future DRAM prices.

watchItems.label: Samsung / SK Hynix DRAM capacity & pricing posture (MANUAL — foreign filers, not on EDGAR/FMP)
watchItems.tripwire: Samsung or SK Hynix announces aggressive DRAM capacity additions, restarts idled fabs, or initiates price cuts to gain share — a supply-side signal that the sector pricing cycle is topping.

watchItems.label: DRAM contract/spot price index, month-over-month (MANUAL — TrendForce/DRAMeXchange monthly print; not on EDGAR/FMP)
watchItems.tripwire: The monthly DRAM contract price index (or spot price) turns DOWN month-over-month — the earliest and purest read that the memory pricing cycle is topping, typically leading Micron's reported ASP decline by a quarter or more.
